text: By those ňĻangelsňļ lined up in ranks,
and those who diligently drive ňĻthe cloudsňļ,
and those who recite the Reminder!
Surely your God is One!
ňĻHe isňļ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, and the Lord of all points of sunrise.
Indeed, We have adorned the lowest heaven with the stars for decoration
and ňĻforňļ protection from every rebellious devil.
They cannot listen to the highest assembly ňĻof angelsňļ for they are pelted from every side,
ňĻfiercelyňļ driven away. And they will suffer an everlasting torment.
But whoever manages to stealthily eavesdrop is ňĻinstantlyňļ pursued by a piercing flare.
So ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ, which is harder to create: them or other marvels of Our creation? <sup foot_note=910>910</sup> Indeed, We created them from a sticky clay. <sup foot_note=911>911</sup>

foot_notes.910: i.e., the angels, the sun, the moon, the stars, and the mountains.

text: In fact, you are astonished ňĻby their denialňļ, while they ridicule ňĻyouňļ.
When they are reminded, they are never mindful.
And whenever they see a sign, they make fun of it,
saying, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but pure magic.
When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?
And our forefathers as well?‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúYes! And you will be fully humbled.‚ÄĚ
It will only take one Blast, <sup foot_note=912>912</sup> then at once they will see ňĻit allňļ.

foot_notes.912: This is when the Trumpet will be blown the second time.

text: They will cry, ‚ÄúOh, woe to us! This is the Day of Judgment!‚ÄĚ
ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision which you used to deny.‚ÄĚ
ňĻAllah will say to the angels,ňļ ‚ÄúGather ňĻallňļ the wrongdoers along with their peers, and whatever they used to worship
instead of Allah, then lead them ňĻallňļ to the path of Hell.
And detain them, for they must be questioned.‚ÄĚ
ňĻThen they will be asked,ňļ ‚ÄúWhat is the matter with you that you can no longer help each other?‚ÄĚ
In fact, on that Day they will be ňĻfullyňļ submissive.
They will turn on each other, throwing blame.
The misled will say, ‚ÄúIt was you who deluded us away from what is right.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=913>913</sup>

foot_notes.913: Other possible translations: 1. ‚ÄúIt was you who used to pressure us.‚ÄĚ 2. ‚ÄúIt was you who tricked us in the name of the truth.‚ÄĚ 3. ‚ÄúIt was you who dissuaded us from doing good.‚ÄĚ 4. ‚ÄúIt was you who deceived us with false oaths.‚ÄĚ

text: The misleaders will reply, ‚ÄúNo! You disbelieved on your own.
We had no authority over you. In fact, you yourselves were a transgressing people.
The decree of our Lord has come to pass against us ňĻallňļ: we will certainly taste ňĻthe punishmentňļ.
We caused you to deviate, for we ourselves were deviant.‚ÄĚ
Surely on that Day they will ňĻallňļ share in the punishment.
That is certainly how We deal with the wicked.
For whenever it was said to them ňĻin the worldňļ, ‚ÄúThere is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah,‚ÄĚ they acted arrogantly
and argued, ‚ÄúShould we really abandon our gods for a mad poet?‚ÄĚ
In fact, he came with the truth, confirming ňĻearlierňļ messengers.
You will certainly taste the painful torment,
and will only be rewarded for what you used to do.
But not the chosen servants of Allah.
They will have a known provision:
fruits ňĻof every typeňļ. <sup foot_note=914>914</sup> And they will be honoured

foot_notes.914: This implies all sorts of delicacies, not just fruits.

text: in the Gardens of Bliss,
facing each other on thrones. <sup foot_note=915>915</sup>

foot_notes.915: Facing each other implies that no one will have anything in their hearts against others.

text: A drink ňĻof pure wineňļ will be passed around to them from a flowing stream:
crystal-white, delicious to drink.
It will neither harm ňĻthemňļ, nor will they be intoxicated by it.
And with them will be maidens of modest gaze and gorgeous eyes,
as if they were pristine pearls. <sup foot_note=916>916</sup>

foot_notes.916: ‚Äė BaiŠłćun makn√Ľn ‚Äô can also mean spotless eggs.

text: Then they will turn to one another inquisitively. <sup foot_note=917>917</sup>

foot_notes.917: It is assumed that they will ask each other about their lives in the world and what led them to Paradise.

text: One of them will say, ‚ÄúI once had a companion ňĻin the worldňļ
who used to ask ňĻmeňļ, ‚ÄėDo you actually believe ňĻin resurrectionňļ?
When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be brought to judgment?‚Äô‚ÄĚ
He will ňĻthenňļ ask, ‚ÄúWould you care to see ňĻhis fateňļ?‚ÄĚ
Then he ňĻand the othersňļ will look and spot him in the midst of the Hellfire.
He will ňĻthenňļ say, ‚ÄúBy Allah! You nearly ruined me.
Had it not been for the grace of my Lord, I ňĻtooňļ would have certainly been among those brought ňĻto Hellňļ.‚ÄĚ
ňĻThen he will ask his fellow believers,ňļ ‚ÄúCan you imagine that we will never die,
except our first death, nor be punished ňĻlike the othersňļ?‚ÄĚ
This is truly the ultimate triumph.
For such ňĻhonourňļ all should strive.
Is this ňĻblissňļ a better accommodation or the tree of Zaqq√Ľm ? <sup foot_note=918>918</sup>

foot_notes.918: The pagans of Mecca used to make fun of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) when he warned them of this tree. They asked, ‚ÄúHow can a tree grow in Hell?‚ÄĚ So Allah revealed this verse in response.

text: We have surely made it a test for the wrongdoers.
Indeed, it is a tree that grows in the depths of Hell,
bearing fruit like devils‚Äô heads.
The evildoers will certainly ňĻbe left toňļ eat from it, filling up their bellies with it.
Then on top of that they will be given a blend of boiling drink.
Then they will ultimately return to ňĻtheir place inňļ Hell.
Indeed, they found their forefathers astray,
so they rushed in their footsteps!
And surely most of the earlier generations had strayed before them,
although We had certainly sent warners among them.
See then what was the end of those who had been warned.
But not the chosen servants of Allah.
Indeed, Noah cried out to Us, and how excellent are We in responding!
We delivered him and his family <sup foot_note=919>919</sup> from the great distress,

foot_notes.919: i.e., the believers in his family.

text: and made his descendants the sole survivors. <sup foot_note=920>920</sup>

foot_notes.920: Noah‚Äôs sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

text: And We blessed him ňĻwith honourable mentionňļ among later generations:
‚ÄúPeace be upon Noah among all peoples.‚ÄĚ
Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.
ňĻForňļ he was truly one of Our faithful servants.
Then We drowned the others.
And indeed, one of those who followed his way was Abraham.
ňĻRememberňļ when he came to his Lord with a pure heart,
and said to his father and his people, ‚ÄúWhat are you worshipping?
Is it false gods that you desire instead of Allah?
What then do you expect from the Lord of all worlds?‚ÄĚ
He later looked up to the stars ňĻin contemplationňļ, <sup foot_note=921>921</sup>

foot_notes.921: His people reportedly made decisions based on constellations, so Abraham ( Ô∑ļ ) pretended to do the same to convince them he was not able to join them at their pagan feast.

text: then said, ‚ÄúI am really sick.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=922>922</sup>
So they turned their backs on him and went away.
Then he ňĻstealthilyňļ advanced towards their gods, and said ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWill you not eat ňĻyour offeringsňļ?
What is wrong with you that you cannot speak?‚ÄĚ
Then he swiftly turned on them, striking ňĻthemňļ with his right hand.
Later, his people came rushing towards him ňĻfuriouslyňļ.
He argued, ‚ÄúHow can you worship what you carve ňĻwith your own handsňļ,
when it is Allah Who created you and whatever you do?‚ÄĚ
They said ňĻto one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúBuild him a furnace and cast him into the blazing fire.‚ÄĚ
And so they sought to harm him, but We made them inferior.
He later said, ‚ÄúI am leaving ňĻin obedienceňļ to my Lord. He will guide me.
My Lord! Bless me with righteous offspring.‚ÄĚ
So We gave him good news of a forbearing son.

Then when the boy reached the age to work with him, Abraham said, ‚ÄúO my dear son! I have seen in a dream that I ňĻmustňļ sacrifice you. So tell me what you think.‚ÄĚ He replied, ‚ÄúO my dear father! Do as you are commanded. Allah willing, you will find me steadfast.‚ÄĚ
Then when they submitted ňĻto Allah‚Äôs Willňļ, and Abraham laid him on the side of his forehead ňĻfor sacrificeňļ,
We called out to him, ‚ÄúO Abraham!
You have already fulfilled the vision.‚ÄĚ Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.
That was truly a revealing test.
And We ransomed his son with a great sacrifice, <sup foot_note=923>923</sup>

foot_notes.923: Allah sent a ram to be sacrificed in place of Ishmael.

text: and blessed Abraham ňĻwith honourable mentionňļ among later generations:
‚ÄúPeace be upon Abraham.‚ÄĚ
This is how We reward the good-doers.
He was truly one of Our faithful servants.
We ňĻlaterňļ gave him good news of Isaac‚ÄĒa prophet, and one of the righteous. <sup foot_note=924>924</sup>

foot_notes.924: The birth of Isaac is announced after the story of sacrifice. So this is a proof that the son who was going to be sacrificed was Ishmael, not Isaac. This is also supported by 11:71, where Sarah is told that she would give birth to Isaac, who would (reach adulthood and) have a son by the name of Jacob.

text: We blessed him <sup foot_note=925>925</sup> and Isaac as well. Some of their descendants did good, while others clearly wronged themselves.
And We certainly showed favour to Moses and Aaron,
and delivered them and their people from the great distress.
We helped them so it was they who prevailed.
We gave them the clear Scripture,
and guided them to the Straight Path.
And We blessed them ňĻwith honourable mentionňļ among later generations:
‚ÄúPeace be upon Moses and Aaron.‚ÄĚ
Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.
They were truly ňĻtwoňļ of Our faithful servants.
And Elias was indeed one of the messengers.
ňĻRememberňļ when he said to his people, ‚ÄúWill you not fear ňĻAllahňļ?
Do you call upon ňĻthe idol ofňļ Ba‚Äôl and abandon the Best of Creators‚ÄĒ
Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?‚ÄĚ
But they rejected him, so they will certainly be brought ňĻfor punishmentňļ.
But not the chosen servants of Allah.
We blessed him ňĻwith honourable mentionňļ among later generations:
‚ÄúPeace be upon Elias.‚ÄĚ
Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.
He was truly one of Our faithful servants.
And Lot was indeed one of the messengers.
ňĻRememberňļ when We delivered him and all of his family,
except an old woman, <sup foot_note=926>926</sup> who was one of the doomed.
Then We ňĻutterlyňļ destroyed the rest.
You ňĻMeccansňļ certainly pass by their ruins day
and night. Will you not then understand?
And Jonah was indeed one of the messengers.
ňĻRememberňļ when he fled to the overloaded ship.
Then ňĻto save it from sinking,ňļ he drew straws ňĻwith other passengersňļ. He lost ňĻand was thrown overboardňļ.
Then the whale engulfed him while he was blameworthy. <sup foot_note=927>927</sup>

foot_notes.927: For abandoning his city without Allah‚Äôs permission.

text: Had he not ňĻconstantlyňļ glorified ňĻAllahňļ, <sup foot_note=928>928</sup>

foot_notes.928: Before being swallowed and while inside the whale ( see 21:87). The lesson is: good deeds are of great help during difficult times.

text: he would have certainly remained in its belly until the Day of Resurrection. <sup foot_note=929>929</sup>

foot_notes.929: The belly of the whale would have become his grave.

text: But We cast him onto the open ňĻshoreňļ, ňĻtotallyňļ worn out,
and caused a squash plant to grow over him. <sup foot_note=930>930</sup>

foot_notes.930: The squash plant provided Jonah with shade and repelled harmful insects.

text: We ňĻlaterňļ sent him ňĻbackňļ to ňĻhis city ofňļ at least one hundred thousand people,
who then believed ňĻin himňļ, so We allowed them enjoyment for a while.
Ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ if your Lord has daughters, <sup foot_note=931>931</sup> while the pagans ňĻprefer toňļ have sons.

foot_notes.931: Some pagan Arabs believed that the angels were Allah‚Äôs daughters. Some of them were ashamed to have daughters and took pride in their own sons.

text: Or ňĻask themňļ if We created the angels as females right before their eyes.
Indeed, it is one of their ňĻoutrageousňļ fabrications to say,
‚ÄúAllah has children.‚ÄĚ They are simply liars.
Has He chosen daughters over sons? <sup foot_note=932>932</sup>

foot_notes.932: Both sexes are equal before Allah, so the verse questions pagan chauvinism.

text: What is the matter with you? How do you judge?
Will you not then be mindful?
Or do you have ňĻanyňļ compelling proof?
Then bring ňĻusňļ your scripture, if what you say is true!

They have also established a ňĻmaritalňļ relationship between Him and the jinn. Yet the jinn ňĻthemselvesňļ know well that such people will certainly be brought ňĻfor punishmentňļ. <sup foot_note=933>933</sup>

foot_notes.933: Or ‚ÄúYet the jinn know well that they themselves will be brought ňĻfor judgmentňļ.‚ÄĚ This is based on the claim of some pagan Arabs that the angels are Allah‚Äôs daughters through female jinn.

text: Glorified is Allah far above what they claim!
But not the chosen servants of Allah.
Surely you ňĻpagansňļ and whatever ňĻidolsňļ you worship
can never lure ňĻanyoneňļ away from Him
except those ňĻdestinedňļ to burn in Hell.
ňĻThe angels respond,ňļ ‚ÄúThere is not one of us without an assigned station ňĻof worshipňļ.
We are indeed the ones lined up in ranks ňĻfor Allahňļ.
And we are indeed the ones ňĻconstantlyňļ glorifying ňĻHis praiseňļ.‚ÄĚ
They certainly used to say,
‚ÄúIf only we had a Reminder like ňĻthose ofňļ earlier peoples, <sup foot_note=934>934</sup>

foot_notes.934: The ‚ÄúReminder‚ÄĚ mentioned here refers to Scriptures such as the Torah and the Gospel. It is commonly used in the Quran as another name for Islam‚Äôs holy book.

text: we would have truly been Allah‚Äôs devoted servants.‚ÄĚ
But ňĻnowňļ they reject it, so they will soon know.
Our Word has already gone forth to Our servants, the messengers,
that they would surely be helped,
and that Our forces will certainly prevail.
So turn away from the deniers for a while ňĻO Prophetňļ.
You will see ňĻwhat will happen toňļ them, and they too will see!
Do they ňĻreallyňļ wish to hasten Our punishment?
Yet when it descends upon them: <sup foot_note=935>935</sup> how evil will that morning be for those who had been warned!

foot_notes.935: lit., when it lands in their courtyards.

text: And turn away from them for a while.
You will see, and they too will see!
Glorified is your Lord‚ÄĒthe Lord of Honour and Power‚ÄĒabove what they claim!
Peace be upon the messengers.
And praise be to Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds.
